title: A unified approach to interpreting model predictions
methodology: Theoretical unification (axiom-satisfaction proofs) + empirical comparison with LIME and DeepLIFT on MNIST and a medical risk-prediction case study.
contributions: Six existing attribution methods (LIME, DeepLIFT, Layer-wise relevance propagation, classic Shapley, QII, Shapley sampling) are special cases of a single 'additive feature attribution' framework.
Within that framework, only Shapley values uniquely satisfy three desirable axioms: local accuracy, missingness, and consistency.
KernelSHAP estimates Shapley values via a weighted linear regression on perturbation samples — model-agnostic and computationally tractable.
weaknesses: The 'unique under the axioms' claim depends on accepting the axioms — see [[aas2021explaining]], [[janzing2020feature]] for causal-inference critiques.
KernelSHAP scales poorly with feature count and is biased under feature dependence — TreeSHAP [[lundberg2020trees]] is the relevant production tool.
emerald_relevance: Foundational citation for EMERALD-AI's entire XAI stack. The axiomatic uniqueness argument is what lets reviewers know SHAP is not just one explainer among many — it is the unique one satisfying its axiom set.
search_query: Lundberg Lee unified approach interpreting model predictions SHAP NeurIPS 2017
notes: Original SHAP paper. Grounded in cooperative game theory. Cited 20k+ times.
